% Generated metadata -- regenerated every run.
% NOTE: iopart.cls has no per-author affiliation-reference macro like
% sn-jnl's \author[refs]{} -- \author{} and \address{} both take a single
% opaque text blob, so numeric refs are baked in as a literal LaTeX
% superscript via format_iopart_superscript (registered by the loader).
% \author{} is called ONCE with every author comma-joined -- calling it
% multiple times would print separate stacked bold blocks, not one byline
% (verified from iopart.cls's \@fauthor definition, which typesets
% immediately rather than deferring to \maketitle).
\title{Anomalous Transport in a Two-Dimensional Electron Gas}
\author{Alice Anderson$^{1,2}$, Bob Baker$^{2}$}
\address{$^{1}$ Department of Physics, University A, City A, Country A}
\address{$^{2}$ National High Field Laboratory, City B, Country B}
\ead{alice.anderson@university-a.edu}
\begin{abstract}
We report anomalous transport signatures in a two-dimensional electron gas and analyze their temperature dependence.
\end{abstract}
% iopart.cls has no native \keywords macro (verified: absent from the class
% source) -- a plain labeled line is the standard community workaround.
\vspace{10pt}\noindent{\it Keywords\/}: transport, two-dimensional electron gas
\maketitle
